Here’s what was set up and how to use it.

## Result

The app is initialized under **`task2/StreamList`** (on Windows the folder may appear as **`Task2/StreamList`**; it’s the same path).

- **React Native** `0.85.0` (React Native CLI, not Expo)
- **TypeScript** by default: `App.tsx`, `tsconfig.json`, `@react-native/typescript-config`, `__tests__/App.test.tsx`
- **Dependencies** installed with **npm** (`package-lock.json` present)

## About your exact command

1. **`--template react-native-template-typescript`**  
   Current CLI **ignores** this template and prints that **TypeScript is the default** since React Native 0.71. You still get a TS project without it.

2. **First run failed**  
   Init failed while copying the template (`template.config.js` not found). That often comes from **which package manager** the CLI uses.

3. **Working command**  
   Init succeeded with:

   ```bash
   npx @react-native-community/cli@latest init StreamList --pm npm
   ```

   run from inside **`task2`**, with **no** `--template` flag.

If you use **Yarn** and prefer it, you can try `init StreamList --pm yarn` instead of `npm`.

## Run the app

From the project folder:

```bash
cd "c:\Users\Rishabh Gupta\Desktop\OTTReact\StreamList\task2\StreamList"
npx react-native start
```

In another terminal (Android emulator or device):

```bash
cd "c:\Users\Rishabh Gupta\Desktop\OTTReact\StreamList\task2\StreamList"
npx react-native run-android
```

**Note:** `package.json` requires **Node `>= 22.11.0`**.

[[SDK 53] Windows - Filename longer than 260 characters error when running android development build · Issue #36274 · expo/expo](https://github.com/expo/expo/issues/36274)\n4. [Don't create long paths during build to avoid failures on Windows · Pull Request #30248 · facebook/react-native](https://github.com/facebook/react-native/pull/30248)\n5. [Working around Long paths limitation on Windows (#33784) · 883a938 · facebook/react-native](https://github.com/facebook/react-native/commit/883a93871cb1fbca4434600a322f63afbba333da)\n\n# React Native Android Windows CMAKE_OBJECT_PATH_MAX Ninja Filename Length Issue\n\n## Problem\n\nWhen building React Native Android apps on Windows using CMake and Ninja, you may encounter a build failure with the error: \"Filename longer than 260 characters\" or \"ninja: error: mkdir(...): No such file or directory\"[1][3]. This occurs because Windows has a default maximum file path length of 260 characters (MAX_PATH limit)[1]. During Android builds, CMake and Ninja generate deeply nested build directories with long auto-generated names, and when combined with your project path, they exceed this 260-character limit[1].\n\n## Solutions\n\n**Option 1: Move your project closer to the drive root (recommended)**[1]\nMove your project to a short path near the root of your drive (e.g., &#96;C:\\myapp\\&#96;) to reduce the total path length of all generated build files.\n\n**Option 2: Enable Win32 long path support on Windows**[1]\nUse the Group Policy Editor to enable long path support systemwide:\n1. Open Group Policy Editor (&#96;gpedit.msc&#96;)\n2. Navigate to Local Computer Policy \u003e Computer Configuration \u003e Administrative Templates \u003e System \u003e Filesystem\n3. Double-click \"Enable Win32 long paths\" and set it to Enabled\n4. Restart your machine\n\nFor Windows Home editions, enable long paths via the registry by setting &#96;HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\FileSystem\\LongPathsEnabled&#96; to &#96;1&#96;[1].\n\n## Note\n\nThis is a known limitation in Ninja that has been addressed in recent versions through long path support in Ninja's manifest[2]. Ensure you're using an updated version of the Android NDK tools that includes Ninja with long path support enabled.",

{
  "markdown": "# CMake/Ninja build error caused by long file paths on Windows\n\nCopy page\n\n[ Copy pageCopy page as Markdown for LLMs ](https://www.nutrient.io/guides/react-native/troubleshooting/windows-path-length-cmake-error/#) [ View as MarkdownView this page as plain text ](https://www.nutrient.io/guides/react-native/troubleshooting/windows-path-length-cmake-error/#) [ Open with ChatGPTExplain the page with ChatGPT ](https://www.nutrient.io/guides/react-native/troubleshooting/windows-path-length-cmake-error/#) [ Open with ClaudeExplain the page with Claude ](https://www.nutrient.io/guides/react-native/troubleshooting/windows-path-length-cmake-error/#) [ Open with GrokExplain the page with Grok ](https://www.nutrient.io/guides/react-native/troubleshooting/windows-path-length-cmake-error/#)\n\nWhen building your React Native app for Android on Windows, you may encounter the following error:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nTask :app:buildCMakeDebug[arm64-v8a] FAILED\n\nC/C++: ninja: error: mkdir(nutrient_sdk_react_native_codegen_autolinked_build/CMakeFiles/react_codegen_nutrient_sdk_react_native_codegen.dir/react/renderer/components/nutrient_sdk_react_native_codegen): No such file or directory\n\n\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## [ ](https://www.nutrient.io/guides/react-native/troubleshooting/windows-path-length-cmake-error/#why-this-happens) Why this happens\n\nWindows has a default maximum file path length of 260 characters (known as &#96;MAX_PATH&#96;). During an Android build, CMake and Ninja generate deeply nested build directories with long auto-generated names. When your project is located several folders deep in your file system, the combined length of your project path and these generated directory names can exceed the 260-character limit. When that happens, Ninja is unable to create the required directories, and the build fails with a misleading “No such file or directory” error — even though the parent directories exist.\n\nThis isn’t caused by your Gradle version or your Nutrient SDK setup; it’s a Windows-specific file system limitation.\n\n## [ ](https://www.nutrient.io/guides/react-native/troubleshooting/windows-path-length-cmake-error/#solution) Solution\n\nThere are two ways to resolve this.\n\n### [ ](https://www.nutrient.io/guides/react-native/troubleshooting/windows-path-length-cmake-error/#option-1-move-your-project-closer-to-the-drive-root-recommended) Option 1: Move your project closer to the drive root (recommended)\n\nThis is the quickest fix. Move your project to a short path near the root of your drive, for example:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nC:\\myapp\\\n\n\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis reduces the total path length of all generated build files and keeps you well within the 260-character limit.\n\n### [ ](https://www.nutrient.io/guides/react-native/troubleshooting/windows-path-length-cmake-error/#option-2-enable-win32-long-path-support-on-windows) Option 2: Enable Win32 long path support on Windows\n\nFor a more permanent fix, you can lift the 260-character limit systemwide by enabling Win32 long path support via the Group Policy Editor:\n\n1. Open the Group Policy Editor by pressing Win-R, typing &#96;gpedit.msc&#96;, and pressing Enter.\n2. Navigate to Local Computer Policy \u003e Computer Configuration \u003e Administrative Templates \u003e System \u003e Filesystem.\n3. Double-click Enable Win32 long paths and set it to Enabled.\n4. Click OK and restart your machine.\n\nAfter restarting, retry your build with &#96;npx expo run:android&#96; (or your equivalent build command).\n\nNote — The Group Policy Editor (&#96;gpedit.msc&#96;) is available on Windows 10 Pro, Enterprise, and Education editions. It isn’t available on Windows 10 Home. If you’re on Windows Home, use Option 1 or enable long paths via the registry: Set &#96;HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\FileSystem\\LongPathsEnabled&#96; to &#96;1&#96;.\n\n---\n\n### Was this helpful?\n\nYES\n\nNO\n\n### Help us improve\n\n0 / 2000 characters\n\nCancel Send\n\n### Thank you for your feedback!\n\n### Something went wrong. Please try again or let us know.\n\nTry Again [Contact Us](https://www.nutrient.io/company/contact/)\n\n---\n\nOn this page\n\n## On this page\n\nASK AI\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"@context\": \"https://schema.org\",\n  \"@type\": \"TechArticle\",\n  \"mainEntityOfPage\": {\n    \"@type\": \"WebPage\",\n    \"@id\": \"https://www.nutrient.io/guides/react-native/troubleshooting/windows-path-length-cmake-error/\"\n  },\n  \"headline\": \"CMake/Ninja build error caused by long file paths on Windows\",\n  \"description\": \"Learn why Ninja fails with a ‘No such file or directory’ error during Android builds on Windows and how to fix it.\",\n  \"inLanguage\": \"en-US\",\n  \"articleSection\": \"Troubleshooting\",\n  \"wordCount\": 322,\n  \"publisher\": {\n    \"@type\": \"Organization\",\n    \"name\": \"Nutrient\",\n    \"logo\": {\n      \"@type\": \"ImageObject\",\n      \"url\": \"https://www.nutrient.io/_astro/nutrient-logo.CJxiofUP_19DKCs.svg\"\n    },\n    \"sameAs\": [\n      \"https://www.nutrient.io/company/about\",\n      \"https://www.linkedin.com/company/nutrientdocs\",\n      \"https://www.facebook.com/nutrientdocs/\",\n      \"https://x.com/nutrientdocs\"\n    ]\n  },\n  \"author\": {\n    \"@type\": \"Organization\",\n    \"name\": \"Nutrient Documentation Team\"\n  }\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;",

}

**Handling cmake paths**

The issue stems from CMake's absolute path encoding in the build process, likely originating from the initial Gradle invocation or package build.gradle configuration.

Enable it (requires Administrator PowerShell), then restart the PC:

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Explaining build failure**

The build failed due to MAX_PATH limitations combined with long paths in the new architecture CMake codegen. </details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is what was going wrong and what we changed.

## Cause

The failure is **Windows’ legacy ~260-character path limit** (`MAX_PATH`), not Gradle or your code. With **React Native 0.85** and **New Architecture** (always on since 0.82; `newArchEnabled=false` is **ignored**), CMake/Ninja compile generated C++ under `node_modules/react-native-safe-area-context`. The **full absolute path** is folded into Ninja’s object paths. Under something like  
`C:\Users\Rishabh Gupta\Desktop\OTTReact\StreamList\Task2\StreamList\...` that goes **past 260 characters**, so Ninja fails with `Filename longer than 260 characters`.

- **`CMAKE_OBJECT_PATH_MAX`** alone did not fix it here (Ninja still hits the limit on those paths).
- **`SUBST` / a short drive letter** did not help either: the toolchain still used the **canonical** `C:\Users\...` path for sources, so paths stayed long.

**Reliable fixes:**

1. **Turn on Win32 long paths** (recommended on your machine — the check script reports they are **off**), **restart**, then build again.  
2. **Or** put the repo on a **short real path** (e.g. `C:\dev\StreamList`) so paths stay under the limit even without long paths.

## What we added in the repo

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `Task2/StreamList/android/app/build.gradle` | Passes `-DCMAKE_OBJECT_PATH_MAX=1024` to CMake (helps in some setups; still enable long paths on Windows as above). |
| `Task2/StreamList/android/gradle.properties` | Short comment block describing the Windows long-path issue and pointing to `npm run android:check-win`. |
| `Task2/StreamList/scripts/verify-windows-long-paths.ps1` | **New** — checks `LongPathsEnabled` and prints exact steps if it’s off. |
| `Task2/StreamList/package.json` | **`android:check-win`** script that runs that verifier. |

## What you should run

1. **Check current setting**

```bash
npm run android:check-win
```

2. **If it says long paths are off**, open **PowerShell as Administrator**, run:

```powershell
New-ItemProperty -Path HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem -Name LongPathsEnabled -Value 1 -PropertyType DWORD -Force
```

3. **Restart Windows**, then from the app folder:

```bash
cd android
.\gradlew.bat clean
cd ..
npx react-native run-android
```

**Alternative:** Move/copy the project to e.g. `C:\dev\StreamList` and open it from there (shorter path, no registry change).

The **safe-area-context manifest** lines in the log are **warnings** from the library, not the cause of this failure. They can be ignored until the library updates.

Tests: **`npm run android:check-win`** was executed; it exited **1** because **`LongPathsEnabled` is not set** on this PC, which matches the build error. After you enable long paths and reboot, the Android build should be re-run locally to confirm.

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is what went wrong and what we changed.

## Why removing `react-native-safe-area-context` was a bad idea

That package is the **only** autolinked native library in this app. After it was removed, generated `Android-autolinking.cmake` had an **empty** `AUTOLINKED_LIBRARIES` list. The CMake step for `appmodules` then **failed to link** the C++ runtime correctly, which produced hundreds of **`ld.lld: undefined symbol: std::__ndk1::...`** errors.

So you **need** `react-native-safe-area-context` for a normal RN 0.85 Android build with this template.

## Why you still see the long-path error

The original failure is unchanged: **Windows’ ~260-character path limit** plus **New Architecture** CMake codegen for `react-native-safe-area-context` under a long path like  
`...\Desktop\OTTReact\StreamList\Task2\StreamList\...`.

That has to be fixed **on the machine**, not by deleting the library.

## What we added

| Item | Purpose |
|------|--------|
| `scripts/enable-long-paths-admin.ps1` | Asks for elevation and sets **LongPathsEnabled** in the registry (same as the manual admin command). |
| `package.json` → **`"enable-long-paths"`** | Runs that script so you can fix the OS limit in one step. |
| `verify-windows-long-paths.ps1` | Updated to mention `npm run enable-long-paths` and moving the project. |
| `App.tsx` + **`react-native-safe-area-context`** | Restored so Android native linking works again. |

**`package.json`** and **`package-lock.json`** are updated after `npm install`.

## What you should do

1. **Run (once), accept UAC, then restart Windows:**

```bash
npm run enable-long-paths
```

2. After reboot, from the app folder:

```bash
cd android
.\gradlew.bat clean
cd ..
npx react-native run-android
```

**Alternative:** Move the whole project to a **short path** (e.g. `C:\dev\StreamList`) and open/build from there **without** relying on long paths.

---

**Files touched:** `Task2/StreamList/App.tsx`, `Task2/StreamList/package.json`, `Task2/StreamList/package-lock.json`, `Task2/StreamList/android/gradle.properties`, `Task2/StreamList/scripts/verify-windows-long-paths.ps1`, new `Task2/StreamList/scripts/enable-long-paths-admin.ps1`.

**Tests:** `npm test` passes.

_**Agent (default)**_


## Why this keeps happening

This is **not a bug in your app code**. On Windows, **Ninja** (used by the Android NDK/CMake build) still hits the **legacy 260-character path limit** when it builds **New Architecture** C++ for libraries like `react-native-safe-area-context`.

The failing path is built from:

1. Your **long project root** (`…\Desktop\OTTReact\StreamList\Task2\StreamList`)
2. Plus **`node_modules\react-native-safe-area-context\...`**
3. CMake/Ninja then **embeds that full absolute path** in generated object paths.

So the combined string is **longer than 260 characters**, and the build fails **again** until the **environment** is fixed.

**Things that do *not* reliably fix it alone:** only tweaking Gradle, only `CMAKE_OBJECT_PATH_MAX`, or only a shorter CMake output folder — we verified the failure still references `C_/Users/.../node_modules/...` in the object name.

**What actually fixes it:**

1. **Turn on Win32 long paths** (recommended), **restart the PC**, then build again — `npm run enable-long-paths` (UAC), or the registry command from `npm run android:check-win`.  
2. **Or** move the whole repo to a **very short path** (e.g. `C:\dev\StreamList`) so even long `node_modules` paths stay under the limit **without** changing the OS setting.

---

## What we changed in the project

| Change | Purpose |
|--------|--------|
| `android/app/src/main/jni/CMakeLists.txt` | Sets `CMAKE_OBJECT_PATH_MAX` before `project()` (supported RN customization). |
| `android/app/build.gradle` | Wires that CMake file, passes `-DCMAKE_OBJECT_PATH_MAX=4096`, and on Windows uses a shorter CMake staging dir under `%USERPROFILE%\rn-cmake\StreamList-cxx`. |
| `scripts/ensure-long-paths.cjs` | On Windows, **blocks** `npm run android` if long paths are off **and** the project path isn’t extremely short — so you get a **clear error** instead of a long Gradle/Ninja failure. |
| `package.json` | `"android"` is now: `node scripts/ensure-long-paths.cjs && react-native run-android`. |
| `android/gradle.properties` | Comment updated to match the real limitation. |

---

## What you should do

1. **Enable long paths and reboot** (one-time):

```bash
npm run enable-long-paths
```

Restart Windows, then:

```bash
npm run android
```

2. **Or** copy the project to something like **`C:\dev\StreamList`**, open that folder in Cursor, then run `npm run android` from there.

**Note:** Android Studio or `gradlew` directly **skips** the npm guard. Prefer **`npm run android`**, or run `node scripts/ensure-long-paths.cjs` before Gradle manually.

---

**Files touched:** `Task2/StreamList/android/app/build.gradle`, `Task2/StreamList/android/app/src/main/jni/CMakeLists.txt` (new), `Task2/StreamList/android/gradle.properties`, `Task2/StreamList/scripts/ensure-long-paths.cjs` (new), `Task2/StreamList/package.json`.

---

Here’s what was wrong and what we changed.

## Why a “version downgrade” helps

The repeated **CMake / Ninja / 260-character path** errors come from **New Architecture** (Fabric) native codegen (e.g. `react_codegen_safeareacontext`). That is **not** fixed only by tweaking Gradle.

**React Native 0.82+ always uses the New Architecture** and **ignores** `newArchEnabled=false`, so you stay on that codegen path.

**React Native 0.81.x is the last line where you can turn the New Architecture off** in `gradle.properties`. With **`newArchEnabled=false`**, the Android build uses the **legacy (Paper) path** for libraries like `react-native-safe-area-context`, so it **does not run that Fabric C++ codegen** and the **Windows path issue goes away** (confirmed: `:app:assembleDebug` **succeeds** without the previous CMake step).

## What we changed

| Area | Change |
|------|--------|
| **Dependencies** | `react-native` **0.85.0 → 0.81.6**, `react` **19.1.4**, `@react-native/new-app-screen` **0.81.6**, CLI packages **19.1.2**, all `@react-native/*` dev tools **0.81.6** (removed non-existent `@react-native/jest-preset` npm package). |
| **`android/gradle.properties`** | **`newArchEnabled=false`** with a short comment that 0.82+ won’t honor this. |
| **`MainApplication.kt`** | Aligned with the **0.81** template: **`DefaultReactNativeHost`** + **`reactNativeHost`**, and **`reactHost`** from **`getDefaultReactHost(applicationContext, reactNativeHost)`** (fixes the Kotlin errors you had after the downgrade). |
| **`android/app/build.gradle`** | Restored a **standard** app Gradle file (removed the extra JNI/CMake staging workarounds). |
| **`jni/CMakeLists.txt`** | **Removed** (not needed for legacy Android). |
| **`package.json` scripts** | **`android`** is again **`react-native run-android`** (no `ensure-long-paths` gate). |
| **`jest.config.js`** | **`preset: 'react-native'`** (uses the preset shipped inside the `react-native` package). |
| **`engines`** | **`node`: `>=20.19.4`** to match RN 0.81.6. |

**Tests:** `npm test` passes. **Android:** `gradlew :app:assembleDebug` completed successfully.

## What you should run

```bash
cd Task2/StreamList
npm install
npx react-native run-android
```

**iOS (if you use it):** from `ios/`, run `pod install` after the version change.

## Trade-offs

- You are on **RN 0.81.6** with **legacy architecture** until you choose to move to **0.82+** and adopt **New Architecture** (and then deal with long paths or a short project path on Windows again).
- Plan a **planned upgrade** to 0.82+ when you’re ready for New Architecture and have long paths enabled or a shorter clone path.

**Files touched:** `Task2/StreamList/package.json`, `package-lock.json`, `jest.config.js`, `android/gradle.properties`, `android/app/build.gradle`, `android/app/src/main/java/com/streamlist/MainApplication.kt`, deleted `android/app/src/main/jni/CMakeLists.txt`, deleted `scripts/ensure-long-paths.cjs`.

## What that screen means

**“Unable to load script”** means the **Android app started**, but it **could not load JavaScript** from the **Metro bundler** (development server on **port 8081**). The project files and `index.js` / `AppRegistry` setup look fine; this is almost always **Metro not running** or **the device not reaching your PC on 8081**.

---

## Fix (do this every time you develop)

### 1. Start Metro first (required)

In a terminal, **from the app folder** (`Task2/StreamList`):

```bash
cd "c:\Users\Rishabh Gupta\Desktop\OTTReact\StreamList\Task2\StreamList"
npm start
```

Wait until you see something like **“Metro waiting on port 8081”** / **“Dev server ready”**. **Leave this terminal open.**

### 2. Run the app again

**Either:**

- Press **Reload** on the red screen (**R** twice in the emulator), **or**
- In a **second** terminal:

```bash
npx react-native run-android
```

**Or** open the app from the emulator again after Metro is up.

---

## If you use a **physical phone** (USB)

Run once per USB session:

```bash
adb reverse tcp:8081 tcp:8081
```

So the phone can reach Metro on your computer.

---

## If it still fails

1. **Nothing else on 8081** – close other Metro/Node apps, or start Metro on another port:
   ```bash
   npx react-native start --port 8082
   ```
   Then run Android with the same port (see React Native docs for `--port` on `run-android`).

2. **Firewall** – allow **Node.js** (or the terminal you use) on **private** networks so port **8081** isn’t blocked.

3. **Cache** – from the project folder:
   ```bash
   npx react-native start --reset-cache
   ```
   Then reload the app.

---

## Why it happens

Debug builds **don’t** embed `index.android.bundle`; they **load it from Metro**. If Metro isn’t running, or the device can’t connect to **8081**, you get exactly this red screen.

**Summary:** Keep **`npm start`** running in one window, then run/reload the app in another (or reload after Metro is ready). No project file change is required for a normal setup.

---

_**User**_

Create a file at `.cursor/rules` with these exact rules:

1. All API calls must go through `src/api/client.ts` only. Never import Axios directly in any hook or component.
2. All color values must come from `src/theme/colors.ts`. No hardcoded hex strings anywhere in component or screen files.
3. All spacing values must come from `src/theme/spacing.ts`. No hardcoded pixel numbers in StyleSheet files.
4. Every screen must fetch data exclusively through a custom hook. No inline `useEffect` + `fetch` or `useEffect` + `axios` inside screen components.
5. Every custom data-fetching hook must return exactly this shape: `{ data, loading, error, refetch }`. No other shape is permitted.
6. No `any` types anywhere. TypeScript strict mode is enforced.
7. No third-party UI component libraries. No React Native Paper, Elements, or similar.
8. No CSS-in-JS. No NativeWind, Styled Components, or similar.
9. No navigation library other than React Navigation v6.
10. No state management library other than Zustand.
11. Never use a 1px solid border at full opacity. Boundaries are created through background color shifts or spacing only.
12. Never hardcode the TMDB API token in any source file. It must come from environment variables only.
13. All navigation params must be typed using the interfaces in `src/navigation/types.ts`. No untyped `navigation.navigate()` calls.
14. No Redux, MobX, or any state library other than Zustand.
15. StyleSheet.create() only for styling. No inline style objects except for dynamic values.

_**User**_

Install babel-plugin-module-resolver as a dev dependency:

npm install --save-dev babel-plugin-module-resolver

After installing, update `babel.config.js` to include the module-resolver plugin so we can use clean import paths like `@theme/colors` instead of `../../theme/colors`.

The babel.config.js should look like this:

module.exports = {
  presets: ['module:@react-native/babel-preset'],
  plugins: [
    ['module-resolver', {
      root: ['./src'],
      extensions: ['.ios.js', '.android.js', '.js', '.ts', '.tsx', '.json'],
      alias: {
        '@api': './src/api',
        '@components': './src/components',
        '@hooks': './src/hooks',
        '@navigation': './src/navigation',
        '@screens': './src/screens',
        '@store': './src/store',
        '@theme': './src/theme',
        '@utils': './src/utils',
        '@assets': './src/assets',
      },
    }],
    'react-native-dotenv',
  ],
};

Show me the updated babel.config.js after making changes.

_**Agent (default)**_


Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed.

## Cause

`@react-native-async-storage/async-storage` **v3+** depends on  
`org.asyncstorage.shared_storage:storage-android:1.0.0`.  
That AAR is **not** on Maven Central; it’s shipped in the npm package under:

`node_modules/@react-native-async-storage/async-storage/android/local_repo`

Gradle never looked at that folder, so resolution failed.

## Fix

**`android/build.gradle`** — after `apply plugin: "com.facebook.react.rootproject"`, an `allprojects` block was added that registers that directory as a Maven repo:

```gradle
apply plugin: "com.facebook.react.rootproject"

// @react-native-async-storage/async-storage v3+ publishes storage-android only in this local Maven repo (not Maven Central).
allprojects {
    repositories {
        maven {
            url(uri("${rootDir}/../node_modules/@react-native-async-storage/async-storage/android/local_repo"))
        }
    }
}
```

`rootDir` is the `android` folder, so `../node_modules/...` correctly points at your app’s `node_modules`.

After this, a local build got past dependency resolution and **`:react-native-async-storage_async-storage:compileDebugKotlin`** ran successfully, so **`storage-android` is resolving**.

## What you should do

From `Task2\StreamList`:

```powershell
cd android
.\gradlew.bat clean
cd ..
npx react-native run-android
```

If anything else fails (e.g. **`react-native-screens`** CMake / linker), say so and we can treat that as a separate issue; it’s unrelated to the missing `storage-android` artifact.

**File updated:** `Task2/StreamList/android/build.gradle`

Reverted the last change: the `allprojects { ... local_repo ... }` block and its comment are removed from `android/build.gradle`. The file again ends right after `apply plugin: "com.facebook.react.rootproject"`.
